channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump says he's considering buying used planes to serve as Air Force One amid Boeing delays
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Wednesday he is considering buying used Boeing aircraft — perhaps from an overseas seller — to use as Air Force One when he's aboard, as he fumes over the U.S. plane-maker's delays in producing two specially modified ones for presidential use.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump backs idea to send some DOGE savings to American citizens
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Wednesday that he likes the idea of giving some of the savings from Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency back to U.S. citizens as a kind of dividend.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Arkansas victims of mortuary theft scheme inspire push for law banning sale of human remains
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — A year had passed since Doneysha Smith suffered a stillbirth and the memories of her loss lingered in 2023.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans are pursuing separate paths to get Trump's priorities through Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Majority Leader John Thune teed up a vote this week on a budget plan that sets the stage for a massive boost in defense and border security spending, and leaves a looming tax cut fight for later this year.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 2 people are dead in a small plane collision at a southern Arizona airport
item.description: A midair collision involving two small planes in southern Arizona killed two people Wednesday morning, authorities said. Federal air-safety investigators said each plane had two people aboard when they collided at Marana Regional Airport on the outskirts of Tucson.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: New, cheaper iPhone 16e has 'best battery life ever,' Apple says
item.description: "iPhone 16e has the best battery life ever on a 6.1-inch iPhone, lasting up to six hours longer than iPhone 11 and up to 12 hours longer than all generations of iPhone SE," Apple said.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Agriculture Department tries to rehire fired workers tied to bird flu response
item.description: The Agriculture Department is scrambling to rehire several workers who were involved in the government's response to the ongoing bird flu outbreak that has devastated egg and poultry farms over the past three years.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: IRS plans to lay off 7,000 probationary workers beginning Thursday, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The IRS will lay off roughly 7,000 workers in Washington and around the country beginning Thursday, a person familiar with the plans told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 things to watch at this year's CPAC
item.description: Conservatives are gathering in National Harbor, Md., this week for the annual Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in what is poised to be a celebration for the political right following President Trump’s election victory in November.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KRON4

item.title: This rookie QB card could be worth $50K if you pull it from a new Topps set
item.description: Denver Broncos rookie quarterback Bo Nix impressed on the field, and now a Colorado sports card store is ready to pay $50,000 to keep a unique card featuring him close to home.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, KRON4

item.title: DOGE notches courtroom wins as Elon Musk crusades to slash federal government
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Although some parts of President Donald Trump ’s agenda are getting bogged down by litigation, Elon Musk ’s Department of Government Efficiency is having better luck in the courtroom.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kennedy Center cancels Pride performance featuring Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington
item.description: The Kennedy Center canceled an upcoming performance set to feature the Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington, which the center said was due to financial constraints and scheduling conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man who admitted to shooting Black teen at wrong door dies before sentencing
item.description: Andrew Lester, a man who pleaded guilty last week to second-degree assault after he shot Ralph Yarl, an unarmed teenage boy in Kansas City in 2023, has died, according to a Clay County prosecutor.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, KTLA 5

item.title: Alabama police department called a 'criminal enterprise' following indictment of 5 officers
item.description: "This is a sad day for law enforcement but a good day for the rule of law," District Attorney Champ Crocker said.
item.dc:creator: Drew Taylor, NewsNation

item.title: Search and rescue saves man buried in Vail Pass recreation area avalanche
item.description: Once crews got to the area, the man, buried in 2 to 3 feet of snow, was spotted in large part due to his avalanche airbag.
item.dc:creator: Greg Nieto, NewsNation

item.title: The white man who pleaded guilty to shooting a Black teen who rang a wrong doorbell dies
item.description: MISSION, Kan. (AP) — An 86-year-old Missouri man has died just days after pleading guilty to a lesser charge in the 2023 shooting of Ralph Yarl, a Black honor student who rang the white man’s doorbell by mistake, prosecutors announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hanceville police chief, 4 officers and spouse arrested in investigation following dispatcher's overdose death
item.description: Autopsy results show a Hanceville dispatcher found dead in his office in August 2024 died from the combined toxic effects of multiple drugs.
item.dc:creator: Logan Sparkman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Seven Chilean men are charged with burglarizing the homes of Mahomes, Burrow and other star athletes
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Seven men from Chile have been charged in Florida federal court with orchestrating burglaries at the homes of prominent professional athletes around the country, according to a criminal complaint.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Polar vortex makes much of US colder than Greenland, but warmth is coming. Then more cold
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The polar vortex hit its peak across much of America on Wednesday, with an icy grip that made Arctic Greenland seem like a toasty vacation spot in comparison. Even Mars has been warmer than North Dakota this week. But there's hope.
item.dc:creator: BY SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What's the key to surviving a plane crash? Skilled crew, strong seats and wings that detach
item.description: Experts say a combination of training and technology were critical to all 76 passengers and four crew members surviving the fiery Delta Air Lines plane crash in Toronto.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Elon Musk finally reveals 'why Memphis' for xAI supercomputer
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- Months after xAI put its operations in Memphis, Elon Musk finally announced why he chose the Bluff City to host the world's largest artificial intelligence supercomputer.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, NewsNation

item.title: Cal Fire captain stabbed, killed in her home
item.description: A Cal Fire captain was fatally stabbed at her home in Ramona, California, Monday in what San Diego County Sheriff's Department officials are investigating as a suspected homicide.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jan. 6 pardon recipients argue it stretches to other crimes: report
item.description: Many defendants are arguing that crimes that were found during the riot investigations should be pardoned.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: Aircrafts collide in midair near Arizona airport; 2 reported dead
item.description: The NTSB said one of the airplanes landed "uneventfully" after the collision, while the other crashed.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Family of DC plane crash victim files $250M claims against FAA, Army
item.description: The claims were filed on behalf of the widow and children of Casey Crafton, 40, of Salem. He died as a result of the crash, leaving behind his wife of 16 years, Rachel, and three young sons.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Schaefer, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: RFK Jr. eyes childhood vaccine schedule review, says 'nothing off limits' in HHS meeting
item.description: Kennedy urged his new staff to drop any preconceived notions of who he is, but also said he wouldn’t stop asking “difficult questions” that have led to “a lot of unpopular conclusions.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Police: Cal Fire captain found stabbed to death inside her home
item.description: An off-duty Cal Fire captain was found dead in her home in what authorities are calling a potential domestic violence incident.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs order to expand White House control of independent agencies
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order aiming to expand the White House’s authority over various independent regulatory agencies, a move likely to prompt legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Medical oxygen in short supply in many parts of world: Research
item.description: A new article published this week in The Lancet medical journal found that more than half of the world's population lacks access to safe and affordable medical oxygen services, and most people outside of high-income countries go without adequate oxygen services for their medical conditions.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: USDA working to rescind terminations for fired officials working on bird flu efforts
item.description: The accidental firings come amid the Trump administration’s sweeping push to reduce the size of the government and cut the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC
